note: Extensible topic taxonomy for the curated-essays layer. Each topic maps to corpus cross-link primitives (a Topics-page keyword, tour slugs, cluster ids) so EssayPage derives related interviews, clips, and tours at render time. Add topics freely; nothing downstream hardcodes this list. corpus_links.keyword feeds /playlist-builder?keywords=; tour_slugs match public/rag/summaries/tours.json; cluster_ids match public/rag/summaries/clusters.json.

topics.label: Family Influence
topics.description: How parents, grandparents, and kin shaped the people who joined the movement, and what the movement asked of families.

topics.label: Intergenerational Activism
topics.description: The transmission of organizing knowledge between older mentors and younger activists, and the debates between generations over strategy.

topics.label: Community Support
topics.description: The local institutions and mutual networks (churches, cooperatives, neighborhoods, benevolent associations) that sustained organizing.

topics.label: Youth and Student Activism
topics.description: Young people and students as organizers, from schoolhouse walkouts to sit-ins and the founding of student movements.

topics.label: Faith and the Church
topics.description: Religious belief and the Black church as both spiritual ground and organizing infrastructure for the movement.

topics.label: Education and Schools
topics.description: Schooling, teachers, citizenship education, and the long struggle over who gets educated and how.

topics.label: Identity and Double Consciousness
topics.description: The interior experience of race in America, the foundational frame Du Bois named double consciousness, which the oral histories revisit in their own words.

topics.label: Music and Culture
topics.description: Song, the sorrow songs, and cultural expression as carriers of memory and as fuel for organizing.

topics.label: Women's Leadership
topics.description: Black women as organizers, educators, and theorists of the struggle, from nineteenth-century essayists to the women who shaped SNCC.

topics.label: Justice and Resistance
topics.description: Confronting racial violence and the law, from anti-lynching journalism to the witness and legal record the movement built.
